title: pastinaca lehmanniana
intro: Pastinaca lehmanniana, also known as giant hogweed or hog's fennel, is a biennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Pastinaca lehmanniana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the plant's roots, stems, or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
